steps.title: Mark the clause boundary
steps.content: The first clause is "They are hardly pristine, though." The next clause begins "many chondrites experience aqueous alteration." Because both clauses are independent, the sentence needs more than a comma between them.

steps.title: Place though correctly
steps.content: Choice A leaves a comma splice. Choice C puts the semicolon before "though," making the transition awkward and improperly punctuated. Choice D uses only commas and still lacks a real clause boundary. The correct punctuation keeps the comma before "though" and uses a semicolon after it. The answer is B.
